case.title: UTAH PHARMACEUTICAL ASSOCIATION v. UNITED STATES

opinion.h1: UTAH PHARMACEUTICAL ASSOCIATION v. UNITED STATES, 371 U.S. 24 (1962)
opinion.h4: UTAH PHARMACEUTICAL ASSOCIATION v. UNITED STATES. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE DISTRICT OF UTAH. No. 277. Decided October 15, 1962.
opinion.p: Arthur B. Hanson and Emmett E. Tucker, Jr. for appellant.
Solicitor General Cox, Assistant Attorney General Loevinger and Lionel Kestenbaum for the United States.
The motion to affirm is granted and the judgment is affirmed.
371 U.S. 24 DAVIDSON v. LAVALLEE, WARDEN. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES COURT OF APPEALS FOR THE SECOND CIRCUIT. No. 446, Misc. Decided October 15, 1962.
The appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
